technology.Artificial Intelligence: Machines performing tasks requiring human-like intelligence. Narrow AI (specific tasks: chess, translation) vs General AI (any task, hypothetical). Approaches: symbolic (rules), ML (data-driven), neural networks.
technology.Machine Learning: Algorithms that improve through experience. Supervised (labeled data: classification, regression), Unsupervised (unlabeled: clustering, dimensionality reduction), Reinforcement (rewards: games, robotics).
technology.Neural Networks: Inspired by brain neurons. Layers: input → hidden → output. Each neuron: weighted sum + activation function (ReLU, sigmoid). Deep learning: many hidden layers. Backpropagation: learn via gradient descent.
technology.Cloud Computing: On-demand computing resources over internet. IaaS (VMs, storage: AWS EC2/S3), PaaS (platform: Heroku), SaaS (apps: Gmail). Three big providers: AWS, Azure, GCP. Serverless: pay per execution.
technology.Cybersecurity: Protecting systems from attacks. Common threats: phishing (deceptive emails), malware (viruses, ransomware), DDoS (overwhelm servers), SQL injection, XSS, MITM. Defense: patching, firewalls, encryption, MFA.
technology.Blockchain: Distributed, immutable ledger. Blocks linked via cryptographic hashes. Consensus (mining/PoS) validates new blocks. Use cases: cryptocurrency (Bitcoin, Ethereum), smart contracts, supply chain tracking.
technology.Containerization: Package app + dependencies into isolated container. Docker: most popular container runtime. Kubernetes: orchestration (deploy, scale, manage). Lighter than VMs (share host OS kernel).
technology.Open Source: Software with source code freely available for use, modification, distribution. Licenses: MIT (permissive), GPL (copyleft: derivative must be open), Apache 2.0 (permissive + patent protection).

history.Industrial Revolution: 1760-1840. Steam engine (Watt), textile machinery, iron production. Shift from manual to machine manufacturing. Led to urbanization, capitalism, modern working class. Second revolution: electricity, assembly line.
history.World War I: 1914-1918. Trigger: assassination of Archduke Ferdinand. Trench warfare, new weapons (machine guns, tanks, chemical gas, aircraft). 16 million deaths. Result: collapse of empires, League of Nations.
history.World War II: 1939-1945. Axis (Germany, Japan, Italy) vs Allies (US, UK, USSR, China). Holocaust (6 million Jews killed). Atomic bombs (Hiroshima, Nagasaki). 70-85 million deaths. Result: UN, Cold War, decolonization.
history.Cold War: 1947-1991. US vs USSR ideological conflict. Nuclear arms race (MAD: mutually assured destruction). Proxy wars (Korea, Vietnam, Afghanistan). Space Race (Sputnik, Apollo 11 moon landing 1969). Fall of Berlin Wall 1989.
history.Internet History: ARPANET 1969 (4 nodes). TCP/IP 1983. World Wide Web: Tim Berners-Lee 1989 (HTML, HTTP, URLs). Mosaic browser 1993. Google 1998, Wikipedia 2001, Facebook 2004, iPhone 2007.
history.Space Race: 1957-1975. USSR: Sputnik (first satellite), Vostok (first human: Yuri Gagarin). US: Apollo program (first moon landing: Neil Armstrong 1969). Motivated by Cold War competition.
history.Ancient Civilizations: Mesopotamia (writing, law code), Egypt (pyramids, hieroglyphs), Indus Valley (urban planning, plumbing), China (silk, paper, gunpowder), Greece (democracy, philosophy), Rome (law, engineering, republic).

geography.Continents: 7: Africa (largest desert: Sahara), Antarctica (coldest, driest), Asia (largest, Mt Everest), Europe (smallest continent after Australia), North America, South America (Amazon rainforest), Australia (island continent).
geography.Oceans: 5: Pacific (largest, deepest: Mariana Trench), Atlantic (second largest), Indian (warmest), Southern (Antarctic), Arctic (smallest, mostly frozen). Ocean currents regulate global climate.
geography.Climate Zones: Tropical (near equator, hot+wet), Arid (deserts, little rain), Temperate (moderate, four seasons), Continental (extreme temps, inland), Polar (cold, ice caps). Koppen classification system.
geography.Natural Resources: Renewable: solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, biomass. Non-renewable: fossil fuels (coal, oil, natural gas), minerals (iron, copper, lithium, rare earth), nuclear (uranium). Peak oil: maximum extraction rate.
geography.Population: World: ~8 billion. Most populous: India (1.4B), China (1.4B), US (335M). Most dense: Monaco, Singapore, Hong Kong. Urbanization: 55% live in cities (growing).

philosophy.Logic: Study of valid reasoning. Deductive (general→specific: all men are mortal, Socrates is a man → Socrates is mortal). Inductive (specific→general: observed swans are white → all swans are white, uncertain).
philosophy.Ethics: Study of right/wrong. Normative ethics: what should we do? Deontology (duty: Kant's categorical imperative), Consequentialism (outcomes: utilitarianism = greatest good for greatest number), Virtue ethics (character: Aristotle).
philosophy.Epistemology: Study of knowledge. What can we know? Rationalism (reason, Descartes: 'I think therefore I am'), Empiricism (experience, Hume: all knowledge from senses), Skepticism (we can't be certain).
philosophy.Metaphysics: Study of reality. What exists? Materialism (only physical), Idealism (mind is fundamental), Dualism (mind and body separate: Descartes), Free will vs determinism. Nature of time, causality, identity.
philosophy.Existentialism: Individual exists first, defines own meaning. Key figures: Kierkegaard, Nietzsche, Sartre, Camus. Themes: freedom, choice, absurdity (meaningless universe), authenticity (being true to self).
philosophy.Political Philosophy: How should society be governed? Plato's Republic (philosopher-kings), Hobbes (social contract: give up freedom for security), Locke (natural rights: life, liberty, property), Marx (class struggle, communism).

economics.Supply and Demand: Price determined by supply (quantity producers offer) and demand (quantity consumers want). Equilibrium: supply = demand. Shortage (demand > supply): prices rise. Surplus (supply > demand): prices fall.
economics.Inflation: General increase in prices over time. Causes: demand-pull (too much money chasing too few goods), cost-push (production costs rise), monetary (money supply increases). Measured by CPI (consumer price index).
economics.GDP: Gross Domestic Product: total value of goods/services produced in a country. C + I + G + NX (Consumption + Investment + Government + Net Exports). Real GDP adjusts for inflation. Per capita: GDP/population.
economics.Market Types: Perfect competition (many small firms, identical products), Monopoly (one firm, no substitutes), Oligopoly (few large firms), Monopolistic competition (many firms, differentiated products).
economics.Cryptocurrency: Decentralized digital currency. Bitcoin (2009): proof-of-work, fixed supply 21M. Ethereum: smart contracts, proof-of-stake. Volatile, speculative. Used for: transfer of value, DeFi, NFTs.
economics.Stock Market: Market for buying/selling company shares. Primary: IPO (company issues shares). Secondary: trading between investors. Indices: S&P 500 (500 large US), Dow Jones (30 blue-chip), NASDAQ (tech-heavy).
economics.Interest Rates: Cost of borrowing money. Central banks (Fed, ECB) set benchmark rates. Low rates → stimulate economy (cheaper borrowing). High rates → fight inflation (more expensive borrowing). Affects everything: mortgages, bonds, stocks.

health.Human Nutrition: Macronutrients: carbohydrates (energy: 4 cal/g), proteins (building blocks: 4 cal/g), fats (energy storage: 9 cal/g). Micronutrients: vitamins (organic: C, D, B12), minerals (inorganic: iron, calcium, zinc).
health.Exercise: Types: aerobic (cardio: running, swimming — improves heart/lungs), anaerobic (weights, sprinting — builds muscle), flexibility (stretching, yoga). WHO recommends 150min moderate or 75min vigorous per week.
health.Sleep: Essential for memory consolidation, immune function, cellular repair. Stages: NREM (light, deep) and REM (dreaming). Adults need 7-9 hours. Sleep deprivation impairs cognition, weakens immune system.
health.Common Diseases: Cardiovascular (heart disease, stroke — #1 killer globally), Diabetes (type 1: autoimmune, type 2: insulin resistance — linked to obesity), Cancer (uncontrolled cell growth), Infectious (COVID-19, influenza, HIV, malaria).
health.Vaccines: Train immune system to recognize pathogens. Types: mRNA (COVID: Moderna, Pfizer), viral vector (J&J, AstraZeneca), inactivated (polio, flu), live attenuated (MMR, chickenpox). Herd immunity when enough vaccinated.
health.Mental Health: Common conditions: depression (persistent sadness, loss of interest), anxiety (excessive worry), PTSD (trauma response), bipolar (mood swings), schizophrenia (psychosis). Treatment: therapy (CBT), medication, lifestyle changes.

internet.Web Browsers: Programs that render web pages. Major: Chrome/Chromium (Blink engine), Firefox (Gecko), Safari (WebKit). Features: tabs, extensions, dev tools. Private browsing doesn't save history. Cookies store site data.
internet.Search Engines: Index web pages for retrieval. Google (~90% market share), Bing, DuckDuckGo (privacy-focused). Crawling: bots follow links to discover pages. Indexing: storing page content. Ranking: relevance algorithms (PageRank).
internet.Social Media: Platforms for user-generated content and social interaction. Major: Facebook (Meta), Instagram (photo), Twitter/X (microblogging), YouTube (video), Reddit (communities), LinkedIn (professional), TikTok (short video).
internet.Email: Electronic mail. Protocols: SMTP (send), IMAP (receive, server keeps messages), POP3 (receive, download+delete). Headers: From, To, Subject, Date. Attachments: MIME encoding. Spam filters block unwanted mail.
internet.Cloud Storage: Files stored on remote servers accessed via internet. Major: Google Drive, Dropbox, iCloud, OneDrive. Sync: changes propagate across devices. Sharing: links with permissions. Version history: recover old versions.
internet.Internet Protocols: Foundation: TCP/IP (packet switching). Application: HTTP (web), SMTP (email), FTP (file transfer), DNS (name resolution), DHCP (IP assignment), TLS/SSL (encryption). Port numbers identify services.

general_knowledge.What the internet is: Global network of interconnected computers communicating via TCP/IP protocols. Decentralized (no single owner). Billions of devices connected. Enables web, email, streaming, gaming, communication.
general_knowledge.What a computer is: Electronic device that processes data under program control. Hardware: CPU, RAM, storage, input/output. Software: OS (manages hardware), applications (user tasks). Four functions: input, process, output, store.
general_knowledge.What electricity is: Flow of electrons through a conductor. Current (I: amperes) = flow rate. Voltage (V: volts) = potential difference. Resistance (R: ohms) = opposition to flow. Ohm's law: V = IR.
general_knowledge.What time is: Fundamental dimension (4th dimension after 3D space). Measured via periodic phenomena (atomic clocks: cesium-133 oscillations). Relativity: time is relative (dependent on gravity and velocity). Direction: entropy always increases (arrow of time).
general_knowledge.What consciousness is: Subjective experience of being. Hard problem: why is there something it's like to be you? Theories: materialism (emergent from brain processes), dualism (non-physical), panpsychism (consciousness fundamental). Not yet scientifically explained.
general_knowledge.What life is: Characteristics: organization (cells), metabolism (energy use), growth, adaptation, response to stimuli, reproduction. Origin: abiogenesis (non-living → living, ~3.5 billion years ago). Definition debated (viruses? prions?).
general_knowledge.What money is: Medium of exchange, store of value, unit of account. Types: commodity (gold, silver), fiat (government-issued, not backed by commodity), digital (cryptocurrency). Functions: facilitates trade beyond barter.
general_knowledge.What language is: System of symbols + rules for communication. Human language: infinite generativity (finite rules produce infinite sentences), displacement (talk about absent things), cultural transmission. 7000+ languages worldwide.
general_knowledge.What music is: Organized sound: melody (pitch sequence), harmony (simultaneous pitches), rhythm (time patterns), timbre (sound quality). Elements: notes, scales, chords, tempo, dynamics. Genres: classical, jazz, rock, pop, electronic, hip-hop.
general_knowledge.What art is: Human creative expression. Forms: visual (painting, sculpture), literary (poetry, novels), performing (theatre, dance), culinary, digital. Purpose: emotional expression, communication, beauty, commentary, decoration.
general_knowledge.What love is: Complex emotion/state. Types: romantic (passion + intimacy), familial (parent-child), platonic (friendship), agape (selfless, universal). Biological basis: oxytocin, dopamine, vasopressin. Subject of art, philosophy, science.
general_knowledge.What happiness is: Subjective well-being. Hedonic (pleasure, absence of pain) vs eudaimonic (meaning, fulfillment). Research: beyond ~$75k/year, more money ≠ more happiness. Factors: relationships, health, autonomy, purpose, gratitude.
